title: The House Spell
subtitle: A folk-magic practice for calling in the right house at the right time
excerpt: A folk-magic practice for the search for a house. Three objects, a written intention, and a fixing-into-place. Used when the search has been long and the wanting needs to be put somewhere outside the head.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The house-blessing practice has many lineages across folk magic and across home-blessing traditions. The form here uses salt, soil, and water as the three elements of a place.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the house search has been long, when the wanting feels stuck in the head, or when a viewing is coming up that matters.

content.text: A folk-magic practice for the house you want. The wanting has been in the head for months. The practice puts it on the kitchen table for twenty minutes and gives it somewhere to live.
New to spell as a practice? Read
how spells work
first.
What this spell is for
The spell is for calling in the right house at the right time. Not a specific house at a specific address. The house that is a good fit for the life you actually have in front of you.
What you will need
A pinch of salt in a small bowl.
A small amount of soil from the garden or a houseplant.
A small glass of water.
A piece of paper and a pen.
A candle.
The spell
Sit at the kitchen table. Place the salt, the soil, and the water in a row in front of you. Light the candle to the right of the row.

On the paper, write three lines. The first line is what the house is for. The second line is what kind of room you want to walk into first. The third line is the feeling you want the house to give you when you put the key in the door.
Read the three lines out loud. Then say: "I am ready for this house. I am ready to live in it well."

Fold the paper into a small square. Touch the corner of the square to the salt, then to the soil, then to the water. The salt is for the kitchen of the house. The soil is for the ground it stands on. The water is for the warmth and the cleaning that will go on inside it.
Put the folded paper somewhere you will see it: a drawer you open every week, a cookbook you use, the back of a frame. Blow the candle out.
Closing
Pour the water down the sink. Return the soil to the garden or the houseplant. The salt can go on the windowsill until the next time you cook with it.
Then go and do the next practical thing in the search. Book a viewing, ring the agent, check the listings. The spell holds the wanting; the practical work brings the house.
Where this practice comes from

Original to homemade.education. The house-blessing has many lineages across folk magic and across home-blessing customs. The version here uses three common kitchen items, so the practice can be done at the table with no special tools.
